name: KeystoneJS
description: GraphQL-native headless CMS for Node.js with a powerful schema-driven approach

description.value: GraphQL-native headless CMS for Node.js with a powerful schema-driven approach

cms-type.comment: API-first headless CMS; no built-in frontend rendering

deployment-model.comment: Self-hosted only; deploy to any cloud provider (Heroku, Digital Ocean, Render, Vercel, etc.)

database-support.comment: PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQLite via Prisma ORM

api-type.comment: GraphQL-native; auto-generated GraphQL API with CRUD, filtering, sorting, pagination

multi-tenancy.comment: Not a built-in feature; would require custom implementation

static-site-generation.comment: JAMstack compatible; works with any static site generator via GraphQL API

edge-deployment.comment: Standard Node.js server; not designed for edge deployment

content-types.comment: Lists define custom content types via code-first schema configuration

custom-fields.comment: 19+ built-in field types plus support for custom field type creation

field-types.comment: Text, Document (rich text), Image/File (media), Relationship, JSON, plus Integer, Decimal, Checkbox, Select, Timestamp, Virtual, and more

content-relationships.comment: Relationship field supports one-to-one, one-to-many, and many-to-many relationships

nested-components.comment: Document field supports nested component blocks within rich text content

localization.comment: No built-in i18n support; on roadmap. Third-party solutions like keystone-multilingual exist

embedded-interactives.comment: Document field supports custom component blocks for interactive content

content-versioning.comment: No built-in version history; feature requested but not implemented

draft-publish.comment: Implemented via select field with draft/published states; not automatic

scheduled-publishing.comment: Timestamp field can store publish dates, but no built-in scheduler; requires custom implementation

approval-workflows.comment: No built-in approval workflows; would require custom implementation

audit-logging.comment: Logging plugin available in v5; can implement via hooks in v6

media-library.comment: Image and File fields with storage configuration

image-transformations.comment: No built-in image transformations; available via Cloudinary integration

external-media-storage.comment: Cloudinary integration for cloud-hosted images; custom storage via configuration

video-support.comment: File field supports any file type including video

folder-organization.comment: No built-in folder organization for media; flat storage

role-based-access.comment: Three-tier access control: operation, filter, and item-level

granular-permissions.comment: Field-level access control with read, create, update permissions

sso-saml.comment: No built-in SSO/SAML; requires third-party integration (Auth0, miniOrange, Passport.js)

team-collaboration.comment: No built-in comments, assignments, or notifications

audience-restrictions.comment: Access control functions can implement any restriction logic

content-type-sdk.comment: Code-first schema definition; content types defined in TypeScript

cli-tools.comment: create-keystone-app CLI for scaffolding; keystone dev/build/start commands

webhooks.comment: afterOperation hooks can trigger external services; not built-in webhooks per se

plugin-system.comment: List plugins and custom field types; less formal than some CMSs

admin-ui-customization.comment: Admin UI built in React; supports custom pages, navigation, components, and branding

documentation-quality.comment: Good documentation with walkthroughs, guides, API reference, and examples

frontend-frameworks.comment: Official Next.js integration with examples; Nuxt.js app available; works with any framework via GraphQL

search-integration.comment: No built-in search; GraphQL filtering only

preview-mode.comment: No built-in preview mode; requires custom implementation

ecommerce-integration.comment: Can build e-commerce via custom schema; no official integrations

cdn-included.comment: Self-hosted only; bring your own CDN

response-caching.comment: No built-in API response caching; implement at infrastructure level

global-regions.comment: Self-hosted; deploy to your preferred regions

free-tier.comment: Fully open source (MIT); self-host for free

pricing-model.comment: 100% free and open source; enterprise support available via Thinkmill

enterprise-features.comment: Paid enterprise plans offer SLA guarantees and dedicated support
